actionable_feedback: {'id': 'fix_sensitivity_magnitude', 'summary': 'Re-run sensitivity analysis using +/-15% perturbations (not +/-5%) and surface row-level classification-change indicators.', 'details': 'Update the workbook to perform threshold perturbation sweeps at -15% and +15% (or otherwise implement the rubric-specified magnitude). Add explicit per-record flags that mark when a record changes class in any perturbation scenario and an aggregated summary table showing counts and which records triggered the needs_review flag.'}

{'id': 'make_spreadsheet_verifiable', 'summary': 'Ensure the spreadsheet is programmatically openable and verifiable; include a formula-validation sheet or snapshots.', 'details': "Save the file as a standard .xlsx compatible with openpyxl and other readers. Add a 'formula_validation' sheet that lists the exact formulas (cell references and formula text) used for: priority calculation, normalized threshold check, post-remediation recalculation, budget feasibility logic, and sensitivity calculations. Optionally include PNG snapshots of key formula cells or a CSV export of computed rows for independent verification."}

{'id': 'justify_drift_exclusion', 'summary': 'Explicitly justify the exclusion of drift_um from the priority aggregation in the change note and in-sheet documentation.', 'details': 'If drift_um is intentionally excluded from the priority formula, add a short rationale (e.g., not mapped to any option driver, known measurement semantics) in both the change note and a cell comment or documentation sheet. If it should be included, update the priority formula and recompute baseline/remediation/sensitivity results.'}

{'id': 'update_change_note_and_regenerate_artifacts', 'summary': 'Update the CNC change note to reflect corrected sensitivity, verifiable formulas, and any recalculated results.', 'details': 'After making workbook changes, regenerate the change note summary values (baseline priorities, post-remediation priorities, crossing counts, recommended bundle) and explicitly cite the worksheet and cell ranges used to compute each reported number. State the corrected sensitivity magnitude and record-level outcomes from the +/-15% sweep.'}

{'id': 'include verification checklist and parse-test', 'summary': 'Add a short verification checklist and a parse-test artifact showing the workbook can be loaded programmatically.', 'details': 'Include a small script or a one-line note indicating the workbook was opened with openpyxl (version) and list the sheets/cell ranges read. If providing environment-specific instructions, mention the openpyxl requirement or include CSV exports of key sheets.'}

Update the sensitivity section to state +/-15% and include direct references to the sheet and cell ranges used for the sweep and any records that changed class.'}

{'artifact': 'attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]', 'comment': "Workbook reportedly contains the computations, but add a 'formula_validation' sheet enumerating formulas and ensure file saved in a standard .xlsx format that parsers (openpyxl) can open. Add per-record sensitivity flags and a summary table."}

feedback_summary: The deliverables meet the artifact contract and contain a clear decision note and reconstructed logic, but two substantive issues reduce semantic confidence: (1) the sensitivity sweep implemented and documented is +/-5% while the rubric requires +/-15%; (2) the workbook could not be programmatically opened/verified in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so in-spreadsheet formulas, row-level indicators, and sensitivity logic are not independently verifiable. Addressing these will restore full rubric compliance and verifier confidence.

recommended_next_steps: Add formula_validation sheet listing formulas and cell references
Ensure workbook opens in openpyxl and include a parse-test record (small script or note)
Regenerate change note and update trace metadata to match artifacts

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model evaluator generated actionable feedback and revision plan.

actionable_feedback.details: Update the workbook to perform threshold perturbation sweeps at -15% and +15% (or otherwise implement the rubric-specified magnitude). Add explicit per-record flags that mark when a record changes class in any perturbation scenario and an aggregated summary table showing counts and which records triggered the needs_review flag.
actionable_feedback.summary: Re-run sensitivity analysis using +/-15% perturbations (not +/-5%) and surface row-level classification-change indicators.

actionable_feedback.details: Save the file as a standard .xlsx compatible with openpyxl and other readers. Add a 'formula_validation' sheet that lists the exact formulas (cell references and formula text) used for: priority calculation, normalized threshold check, post-remediation recalculation, budget feasibility logic, and sensitivity calculations. Optionally include PNG snapshots of key formula cells or a CSV export of computed rows for independent verification.
actionable_feedback.summary: Ensure the spreadsheet is programmatically openable and verifiable; include a formula-validation sheet or snapshots.

actionable_feedback.details: If drift_um is intentionally excluded from the priority formula, add a short rationale (e.g., not mapped to any option driver, known measurement semantics) in both the change note and a cell comment or documentation sheet. If it should be included, update the priority formula and recompute baseline/remediation/sensitivity results.
actionable_feedback.summary: Explicitly justify the exclusion of drift_um from the priority aggregation in the change note and in-sheet documentation.

actionable_feedback.details: After making workbook changes, regenerate the change note summary values (baseline priorities, post-remediation priorities, crossing counts, recommended bundle) and explicitly cite the worksheet and cell ranges used to compute each reported number. State the corrected sensitivity magnitude and record-level outcomes from the +/-15% sweep.
actionable_feedback.summary: Update the CNC change note to reflect corrected sensitivity, verifiable formulas, and any recalculated results.

actionable_feedback.details: Include a small script or a one-line note indicating the workbook was opened with openpyxl (version) and list the sheets/cell ranges read. If providing environment-specific instructions, mention the openpyxl requirement or include CSV exports of key sheets.
actionable_feedback.summary: Add a short verification checklist and a parse-test artifact showing the workbook can be loaded programmatically.

artifact_specific_comments.comment: Change note is well-structured and documents assumptions. Update the sensitivity section to state +/-15% and include direct references to the sheet and cell ranges used for the sweep and any records that changed class.
